prompt:
You receive an argument from a debate on "Abortion".
Check whether it bundles multiple distinct claims into one sentence.
If yes, split it into atomic arguments (one claim each), preserving the stance.
If it already expresses a single claim, return it unchanged.

Important: Only promote claims the speaker actually ASSERTS as their own.
- Concessive/contrastive clauses (markers: "but", "although", "while", "despite",
  "even though", "yet") are rhetorical hedges, NOT independent arguments. Keep them
  attached to the main claim or drop them — never make them a separate atom.
- Reported or conceded positions of the opposing side (e.g. "opponents argue X",
  "greens always argue X", "some claim X, but ...") are NOT the speaker's own claim.
  Do NOT promote them to a separate atom; drop them.
This prevents atoms that, read in isolation, carry the OPPOSITE stance of the speaker
(e.g. a pro-nuclear sentence must never yield a pro-renewable atom).

Self-containment rule: Every output argument MUST be understandable without
any surrounding context. If a fragment contains pronouns like "it", "them",
"this", "they" without a clear referent, replace the pronoun with the explicit
noun from the original sentence. If the referent cannot be recovered, drop
the fragment entirely.

Argument [pro7] (pro):
" If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability , it may go so far as to pro-scribe abortion during that period , except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother . "

Respond ONLY with a JSON array, no markdown. Use IDs like "pro7", "pro7b", "pro7c", ...:
[
  {"id": "pro7",  "text": "...", "stance": "pro"},
  {"id": "pro7b", "text": "...", "stance": "pro"}
]
raw_response: [{"id":"pro7","text":"If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, the State may go so far as to proscribe abortion during the period after viability, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.","stance":"pro"}]

parsed.text: If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, the State may go so far as to proscribe abortion during the period after viability, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.

prompt:
Propose attack candidates for the following argument pairs from a debate on "Abortion".

An attack exists when one argument:
- asserts a conclusion logically incompatible with the other's conclusion — the one claim can be true only if the other is false (rebuttal), OR
- undermines a premise the other relies on (undermining), OR
- shows the other's conclusion does not follow even if its premise is true (undercutting)

Pair pro29:con140b
  A=[pro29] (pro) The World Health Organization estimated in 2004 that unsafe abortions cause 68,000 maternal deaths worldwide each year.
  B=[con140b] (con) Susan B. Anthony and Elizabeth Cady Stanton spoke out against abortion.

  A=[pro29] (pro) The World Health Organization estimated in 2004 that unsafe abortions cause 68,000 maternal deaths worldwide each year.
  B=[con23] (con) I will always support legislation that would end abortion or lead us in the direction of ending abortion.

Pair pro29:con152
  A=[pro29] (pro) The World Health Organization estimated in 2004 that unsafe abortions cause 68,000 maternal deaths worldwide each year.
  B=[con152] (con) For this reason, I introduced S. 583, the Life at Conception Act on March 14, 2013.

  A=[pro29] (pro) The World Health Organization estimated in 2004 that unsafe abortions cause 68,000 maternal deaths worldwide each year.
  B=[con109] (con) Half of all women having abortions each year have had at least one previous abortion, according to the Guttmacher Institute.

  A=[pro29] (pro) The World Health Organization estimated in 2004 that unsafe abortions cause 68,000 maternal deaths worldwide each year.
  B=[con109b] (con) In 2010, 8.5 % of abortions reported to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention were performed on women who had three or more previous abortions.

Pair pro29:con8
  A=[pro29] (pro) The World Health Organization estimated in 2004 that unsafe abortions cause 68,000 maternal deaths worldwide each year.
  B=[con8] (con) Fetuses feel pain during the abortion procedure.

  A=[pro29] (pro) The World Health Organization estimated in 2004 that unsafe abortions cause 68,000 maternal deaths worldwide each year.
  B=[con24] (con) Abortion causes overwhelming guilt.

Pair pro29:con24b
  A=[pro29] (pro) The World Health Organization estimated in 2004 that unsafe abortions cause 68,000 maternal deaths worldwide each year.

prompt:
Propose attack candidates for the following argument pairs from a debate on "Abortion".

An attack exists when one argument:
- asserts a conclusion logically incompatible with the other's conclusion — the one claim can be true only if the other is false (rebuttal), OR
- undermines a premise the other relies on (undermining), OR
- shows the other's conclusion does not follow even if its premise is true (undercutting)

Pair pro7:con8
  A=[pro7] (pro) If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, the State may go so far as to proscribe abortion during the period after viability, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.
  B=[con8] (con) Fetuses feel pain during the abortion procedure.

Pair pro7:con24
  A=[pro7] (pro) If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, the State may go so far as to proscribe abortion during the period after viability, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.
  B=[con24] (con) Abortion causes overwhelming guilt.

Pair pro7:con24b
  A=[pro7] (pro) If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, the State may go so far as to proscribe abortion during the period after viability, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.
  B=[con24b] (con) Abortion causes nightmares.

Pair pro7:con24c
  A=[pro7] (pro) If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, the State may go so far as to proscribe abortion during the period after viability, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.
  B=[con24c] (con) Abortion leads to excessive drinking.

Pair pro7:con24d
  A=[pro7] (pro) If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, the State may go so far as to proscribe abortion during the period after viability, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.
  B=[con24d] (con) Abortion leads to drug abuse.

Pair pro7:con24e
  A=[pro7] (pro) If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, the State may go so far as to proscribe abortion during the period after viability, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.
  B=[con24e] (con) Abortion leads to promiscuity.

Pair pro7:con24f
  A=[pro7] (pro) If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, the State may go so far as to proscribe abortion during the period after viability, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.
  B=[con24f] (con) Abortion causes an inability to form or maintain relationships.

Pair pro7:con24g
  A=[pro7] (pro) If the State is interested in protecting fetal life after viability, the State may go so far as to proscribe abortion during the period after viability, except when it is necessary to preserve the life or health of the mother.
  B=[con24g] (con) Abortion causes difficulty bonding with later children.

Pair pro71:pro63
  A=[pro71] (pro) Americans are more accepting of abortions that occur early in pregnancy when the fetus is not as developed and is not “viable” (able to survive on its own outside of the woman’s body).
  B=[pro63] (pro) Not all abortions are unjustified.

Pair pro71:pro63b
  A=[pro71] (pro) Americans are more accepting of abortions that occur early in pregnancy when the fetus is not as developed and is not “viable” (able to survive on its own outside of the woman’s body).
  B=[pro63b] (pro) Abortion would be justified if the same justification could be applied to killing an adult human.

direction    = exactly one of: A_ATTACKS_B / B_ATTACKS_A / NONE
attack_type  = one of: rebuttal / undercutting / undermining / NONE
  rebuttal:     the two conclusions are logically incompatible — they negate the SAME
                proposition, so one claim is true only if the other is false. Merely
                taking the opposing stance, addressing a different sub-topic, or
                "arguing for the other side" is NOT a rebuttal; there must be a direct
                contradiction between the two specific claims. A descriptive/empirical
                claim (what people believe or do, statistics, trends) and a normative
                claim (what ought to be) are NOT logically incompatible — they cannot
                rebut each other (at most undermining/undercutting). A rebuttal is
                inherently MUTUAL (it holds in both directions); pick either direction.
  undercutting: attacker shows target's conclusion doesn't follow even if target's premise holds
  undermining:  attacker directly attacks a premise the target relies on
  If direction=NONE, attack_type MUST be NONE.
confidence   = integer 0-100: probability that ANY attack exists between the two arguments
  (0 = definitely no attack, 100 = definitely attack)
  If direction=NONE, confidence MUST be 0.

Respond ONLY with a JSON array, same order as the pairs above, no markdown:
[
  {"pair": "id_a:id_b", "direction": "A_ATTACKS_B", "attack_type": "rebuttal", "confidence": 85, "reason": "one sentence"},
  ...
]

parsed.reason: The claim about breast‑cancer risk does not address the legal premise or conclusion of pro7.
The statement refuting a health claim is unrelated to the legal argument about post‑viability abortion restrictions.
Again, a medical refutation does not challenge the premise or conclusion of the pro7 position.
Pro27’s conclusion that the Constitution does not define a fetus as a person attacks the legal premise that the State can protect fetal life after viability.
A historical note about women’s rights does not conflict with or undermine the pro7 argument.
Stating that historical figures opposed abortion does not attack the pro7 claim; it may even support it.
An expression of personal support for ending abortion does not contradict or undermine the pro7 position.
Introducing a more restrictive bill does not attack the pro7 argument about post‑viability restrictions.
Statistics on repeat abortions are unrelated to the legal premise of protecting fetal life after viability.
Data on multiple prior abortions does not challenge the pro7 conclusion about state regulation after viability.

An attack exists when one argument:
- asserts a conclusion logically incompatible with the other's conclusion — the one claim can be true only if the other is false (rebuttal), OR
- undermines a premise the other relies on (undermining), OR
- shows the other's conclusion does not follow even if its premise is true (undercutting)

Pair pro71:pro58
  A=[pro71] (pro) Americans are more accepting of abortions that occur early in pregnancy when the fetus is not as developed and is not “viable” (able to survive on its own outside of the woman’s body).

  B=[pro36a] (pro) The National Cancer Institute refuted the claim that abortion can lead to a higher probability of developing breast cancer.

  A=[pro71] (pro) Americans are more accepting of abortions that occur early in pregnancy when the fetus is not as developed and is not “viable” (able to survive on its own outside of the woman’s body).
  B=[pro36b] (pro) The American Cancer Society refuted the claim that abortion can lead to a higher probability of developing breast cancer.

Pair pro71:pro36c
  A=[pro71] (pro) Americans are more accepting of abortions that occur early in pregnancy when the fetus is not as developed and is not “viable” (able to survive on its own outside of the woman’s body).
  B=[pro36c] (pro) The American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists refuted the claim that abortion can lead to a higher probability of developing breast cancer.

Pair pro71:pro27
  A=[pro71] (pro) Americans are more accepting of abortions that occur early in pregnancy when the fetus is not as developed and is not “viable” (able to survive on its own outside of the woman’s body).
  B=[pro27] (pro) They also ruled that the word "person" in the Constitution did not include a fetus.

Pair pro71:con140

Pair pro71:con140b

Pair pro71:con23

  B=[con23] (con) I will always support legislation that would end abortion or lead us in the direction of ending abortion.

Pair pro71:con152

Pair pro71:con109
  A=[pro71] (pro) Americans are more accepting of abortions that occur early in pregnancy when the fetus is not as developed and is not “viable” (able to survive on its own outside of the woman’s body).

direction    = exactly one of: A_ATTACKS_B / B_ATTACKS_A / NONE
attack_type  = one of: rebuttal / undercutting / undermining / NONE
  rebuttal:     the two conclusions are logically incompatible — they negate the SAME
                proposition, so one claim is true only if the other is false. Merely
                taking the opposing stance, addressing a different sub-topic, or
                "arguing for the other side" is NOT a rebuttal; there must be a direct
                contradiction between the two specific claims. A descriptive/empirical
                claim (what people believe or do, statistics, trends) and a normative
                claim (what ought to be) are NOT logically incompatible — they cannot
                rebut each other (at most undermining/undercutting). A rebuttal is
                inherently MUTUAL (it holds in both directions); pick either direction.
  undercutting: attacker shows target's conclusion doesn't follow even if target's premise holds
  undermining:  attacker directly attacks a premise the target relies on
  If direction=NONE, attack_type MUST be NONE.
confidence   = integer 0-100: probability that ANY attack exists between the two arguments
  (0 = definitely no attack, 100 = definitely attack)
  If direction=NONE, confidence MUST be 0.

Both statements are supportive of abortion and do not contradict each other.

prompt:
Propose attack candidates for the following argument pairs from a debate on "Abortion".

An attack exists when one argument:
- asserts a conclusion logically incompatible with the other's conclusion — the one claim can be true only if the other is false (rebuttal), OR
- undermines a premise the other relies on (undermining), OR
- shows the other's conclusion does not follow even if its premise is true (undercutting)

Pair pro58:pro27
  A=[pro58] (pro) Abortion reduces crime.
  B=[pro27] (pro) They also ruled that the word "person" in the Constitution did not include a fetus.

Pair pro58:con140
  A=[pro58] (pro) Abortion reduces crime.
  B=[con140] (con) Susan B. Anthony and Elizabeth Cady Stanton led the fight for the right of women to own property, to vote, and obtain equal education.

Pair pro58:con140b
  A=[pro58] (pro) Abortion reduces crime.
  B=[con140b] (con) Susan B. Anthony and Elizabeth Cady Stanton spoke out against abortion.

Pair pro58:con23
  A=[pro58] (pro) Abortion reduces crime.
  B=[con23] (con) I will always support legislation that would end abortion or lead us in the direction of ending abortion.

Pair pro58:con152
  A=[pro58] (pro) Abortion reduces crime.
  B=[con152] (con) For this reason, I introduced S. 583, the Life at Conception Act on March 14, 2013.

Pair pro58:con109
  A=[pro58] (pro) Abortion reduces crime.
  B=[con109] (con) Half of all women having abortions each year have had at least one previous abortion, according to the Guttmacher Institute.

Pair pro58:con109b
  A=[pro58] (pro) Abortion reduces crime.
  B=[con109b] (con) In 2010, 8.5 % of abortions reported to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention were performed on women who had three or more previous abortions.

Pair pro58:con8
  A=[pro58] (pro) Abortion reduces crime.
  B=[con8] (con) Fetuses feel pain during the abortion procedure.

Pair pro58:con24
  A=[pro58] (pro) Abortion reduces crime.
  B=[con24] (con) Abortion causes overwhelming guilt.

Pair pro58:con24b
  A=[pro58] (pro) Abortion reduces crime.
  B=[con24b] (con) Abortion causes nightmares.

direction    = exactly one of: A_ATTACKS_B / B_ATTACKS_A / NONE
attack_type  = one of: rebuttal / undercutting / undermining / NONE
  rebuttal:     the two conclusions are logically incompatible — they negate the SAME
                proposition, so one claim is true only if the other is false. Merely
                taking the opposing stance, addressing a different sub-topic, or
                "arguing for the other side" is NOT a rebuttal; there must be a direct
                contradiction between the two specific claims. A descriptive/empirical
                claim (what people believe or do, statistics, trends) and a normative
                claim (what ought to be) are NOT logically incompatible — they cannot
                rebut each other (at most undermining/undercutting). A rebuttal is
                inherently MUTUAL (it holds in both directions); pick either direction.
  undercutting: attacker shows target's conclusion doesn't follow even if target's premise holds
  undermining:  attacker directly attacks a premise the target relies on
  If direction=NONE, attack_type MUST be NONE.
confidence   = integer 0-100: probability that ANY attack exists between the two arguments
  (0 = definitely no attack, 100 = definitely attack)
  If direction=NONE, confidence MUST be 0.

Respond ONLY with a JSON array, same order as the pairs above, no markdown:
[
  {"pair": "id_a:id_b", "direction": "A_ATTACKS_B", "attack_type": "rebuttal", "confidence": 85, "reason": "one sentence"},
  ...
]

prompt:
Propose attack candidates for the following argument pairs from a debate on "Abortion".

An attack exists when one argument:
- asserts a conclusion logically incompatible with the other's conclusion — the one claim can be true only if the other is false (rebuttal), OR
- undermines a premise the other relies on (undermining), OR
- shows the other's conclusion does not follow even if its premise is true (undercutting)

Pair con8:con24f
  A=[con8] (con) Fetuses feel pain during the abortion procedure.
  B=[con24f] (con) Abortion causes an inability to form or maintain relationships.

Pair con8:con24g
  A=[con8] (con) Fetuses feel pain during the abortion procedure.
  B=[con24g] (con) Abortion causes difficulty bonding with later children.

direction    = exactly one of: A_ATTACKS_B / B_ATTACKS_A / NONE
attack_type  = one of: rebuttal / undercutting / undermining / NONE
  rebuttal:     the two conclusions are logically incompatible — they negate the SAME
                proposition, so one claim is true only if the other is false. Merely
                taking the opposing stance, addressing a different sub-topic, or
                "arguing for the other side" is NOT a rebuttal; there must be a direct
                contradiction between the two specific claims. A descriptive/empirical
                claim (what people believe or do, statistics, trends) and a normative
                claim (what ought to be) are NOT logically incompatible — they cannot
                rebut each other (at most undermining/undercutting). A rebuttal is
                inherently MUTUAL (it holds in both directions); pick either direction.
  undercutting: attacker shows target's conclusion doesn't follow even if target's premise holds
  undermining:  attacker directly attacks a premise the target relies on
  If direction=NONE, attack_type MUST be NONE.
confidence   = integer 0-100: probability that ANY attack exists between the two arguments
  (0 = definitely no attack, 100 = definitely attack)
  If direction=NONE, confidence MUST be 0.

Respond ONLY with a JSON array, same order as the pairs above, no markdown:
[
  {"pair": "id_a:id_b", "direction": "A_ATTACKS_B", "attack_type": "rebuttal", "confidence": 85, "reason": "one sentence"},
  ...
]

parsed.reason: Both arguments present separate negative consequences of abortion and do not contradict or undermine each other's premises or conclusions.
The claims address different harms (fetal pain vs later bonding difficulty) and do not logically conflict or attack each other's premises.
